title: Formative Test 1 — English Grammar (M.1)
description: Warm-up test on singular & plural nouns, demonstratives, imperatives, present tenses, and comparative & superlative adjectives.

sections.title: Grammar Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the best answer to complete each sentence.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: I have two ______ in my school bag.
questions.explanation: Regular plural nouns add -s: two books.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: ______ is my sister. She is ten years old.
questions.explanation: Use the singular demonstrative 'This' for one person near the speaker.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: ______ the door, please. It is cold outside.
questions.explanation: Imperatives use the base form of the verb: Close the door.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Look! The baby ______ .
questions.explanation: Use the present continuous (is + -ing) for actions happening now.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: She usually ______ to school by bus.
questions.explanation: 'Usually' signals a routine → present simple, third person singular: goes.

sections.title: Grammar in Context
sections.instructions: Directions: Read the short passage and choose the correct word for each blank.

questions.question: My brother is ______ (6) than me.
questions.explanation: Short adjectives form the comparative with -er: taller than.

questions.question: Mount Everest is the ______ (7) mountain in the world.
questions.explanation: Short adjectives form the superlative with 'the' + -est: the highest.

questions.question: This book is ______ (8) interesting than that one.
questions.explanation: Longer adjectives use 'more' for the comparative: more interesting than.

sections.title: Error Identification
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the underlined part that is grammatically incorrect.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: There (A) are (B) three (C) child (D) in the room.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: She (A) is (B) watch (C) TV (D) now.
questions.explanation: The time marker 'now' requires the present continuous: is watching.

sections.title: Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the word that best completes each sentence.

questions.question: An elephant is ______ than a dog.
questions.explanation: Comparative of 'big' doubles the consonant and adds -er: bigger.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: Please pass me ______ pencils on your desk.
questions.explanation: 'Pencils' is plural and far from the speaker → 'those'.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Right now, I ______ my homework in my room.

sections.title: Reading Comprehension
sections.instructions: Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

passages.title: In the Classroom
passages.passage: It is Monday morning. The students are sitting in their classroom. Mr. Somsak, the English teacher, is writing on the board. 'Open your books to page ten,' he says. 'Read the story quietly.' Two boys are looking out the window at the birds. There are three pictures on the wall. These pictures show animals from the zoo.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: What are the students doing right now?
choices: They are sitting in the classroom.
They are playing in the garden.
questions.answer: They are sitting in the classroom.
questions.explanation: The passage says 'The students are sitting in their classroom' (present continuous).

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: How many pictures are on the wall?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'There are three pictures on the wall' — 'pictures' is a plural noun.
